widgets_values: Hello there, this is a training sample.

nodes.title: Language to Transcribe when audio is not empty

widgets_values: Here we try to segment your audio into many parts by detecting silence and inverting the selected regions.

I'm making sure we output at least 10 segments (if there isn't enough I'm adding empty ones).
> 10 is the amount of segments I'll evaluate on the next step to add to the dataset for training
For a test, choose your character speaking for at least 4 minutes. We will try to segment the audio into clips to use for training a MOSS LoRA.

This Subgaph is evaluating and collecting the previous 10 segments to create a clip dataset + using Qwen3 ASR to get the corresponding transcriptions. 

These collected clips are saved in the ComfyUI/input folder.

It will NOT use audio segments with less than 2.5 seconds or with more than 20 seconds. Those will be ignored by the training collected clips.

Here is where we create a "manifest" json file. This is the "config/path" file that tells the trainer what the clips are, where they are, their transcriptions and properties. 

I know this is not a good UX, I hope to make one that is easier and more firendly in the future.

If everything went right so far, this is where your MOSS LoRA training should happen.

If you have a 24GB you can try to not use 4bit quantization. If you have less, try with qauntization option ON.

For now only 8B model is supported. And only LoRA, no finetune.
Oi, meu nome é Bob, sou uma pessoa real... ou será que sou?

Here is where we create a "manifest" json file. This is the "config/path" file that tells the trainer what the clips are, where they are, their transcriptions and properties. 

I know this is not a good UX, I hope to make one that is easier and more firendly in the future.

If everything went fine you can either connect this to the "dataset_source" input on the "📦 MOSS Dataset Prep" node here on the right. 

OR you can just type yout manifest path there on that node. After your dataset clip and manifest is done, you don't need to run these nodes on the blue group again

# Using your trained LoRA

- Your finished LoRA is moved to the MOSS LoRA folder automatically: models\TTS\moss_tts\loras
- refresh with "R" to see them
- Choose them on the local_lora_adapter

MOSS works without a voice reference (narrator). 

Maybe if you train long enough you wouldn't need a reference when using your LoRA.

But for me, to get to resemblance and correct Brazilian accent, I had to also use my voice as reference + the trained LoRA.

You will end up with less than 10 clips if that is the case. Check the preview text here. Each line is a clip. If it has empty lines, each empty line is a clip that didn't met the criteria.

I'm not sure what is bet to train a MOSS LoRa, this is just me guessing and using ComfyUI to automatically build a dataset. You can and should do this manually.

groups.title: Collect 10 segments to create a clip dataset + use Qwen3 ASR to get the transcription
Prepere your MOSS Datset for training
